name: Ascend SQLite Database Layer
description: SQLite database layer for the Ascend engineering management CLI. Provides connection management, schema initialization with versioning, and health checks for a database storing teams, members, meetings, goals, performance snapshots, coaching entries, and schedules. Implements FTS5 full-text search on meeting transcripts.

types.name: HealthCheckResult

fields.name: ok
fields.description: True if database is healthy and accessible

fields.name: error
fields.description: Error message if ok is False

fields.name: version
fields.description: Current schema version if ok is True

fields.name: tables
fields.description: Mapping of table names to row counts if ok is True

types.description: Health check result dictionary returned by check_db

functions.name: get_connection
functions.description: Opens a SQLite connection to the specified database path with WAL mode and foreign keys enabled. Sets row_factory to sqlite3.Row for dict-like row access.

inputs.name: db_path
inputs.description: Path to the SQLite database file

error_cases.name: database_error
error_cases.condition: If SQLite cannot open or configure the database

postconditions: Connection has row_factory set to sqlite3.Row
Connection has WAL mode enabled (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL)
Connection has foreign keys enabled (PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON)
side_effects: Opens a file connection to the database
Executes PRAGMA statements to configure connection

structured_side_effects.description: Configures connection with WAL mode and foreign keys

functions.name: init_db
functions.description: Creates the database and applies the schema if needed. Checks the current schema version and upgrades to SCHEMA_VERSION (2) if necessary. Creates parent directories if they don't exist. Returns an open connection to the initialized database.

inputs.name: db_path
inputs.description: Path where the database should be created or initialized

error_cases.name: permission_error
error_cases.condition: If parent directory cannot be created due to insufficient permissions

error_cases.name: database_error
error_cases.condition: If SQLite cannot create tables or execute schema SQL

postconditions: Schema version is at least SCHEMA_VERSION (2)
Returns an open sqlite3.Connection with WAL mode and foreign keys enabled
Changes are committed to the database
side_effects: Creates parent directories if they don't exist
Executes schema SQL if version is outdated

structured_side_effects.description: Executes schema DDL and version updates

functions.name: check_db
functions.description: Health check function that returns table counts and schema version. Returns a dictionary with ok, version, and tables keys on success, or ok and error keys on failure. Closes the connection before returning.

inputs.name: db_path
inputs.description: Path to the database to check

error_cases.name: schema_not_initialized
error_cases.condition: If schema_version table does not exist (OperationalError)

postconditions: If ok is False, dict contains 'error': str
If ok is True, dict contains 'version': int and 'tables': dict[str, int]
Tables checked: members, member_flags, teams, team_members, meetings, meeting_items, goals, performance_snapshots, coaching_entries, schedules
side_effects: Executes SELECT queries on schema_version and all listed tables

invariants: _SCHEMA_SQL contains DDL for: schema_version, teams, members, member_flags, team_members, meetings, meetings_fts, meeting_items, goals, performance_snapshots, coaching_entries, schedules
All connections have WAL mode enabled
All connections have foreign keys enabled
All connections use sqlite3.Row as row_factory

data_access.rationale: Manages SQLite database for Ascend engineering management CLI

side_effects.rationale: Reads schema version and table counts for health checks
Creates and initializes database schema, inserts version records
Creates database files and parent directories
